message:
🌱 Add litellm: [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise' mission (#1192)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "litellm-10349-bug-modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-enterprise",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "litellm: [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'",
+    "description": "[Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'. This issue affects 21+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review litellm configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm\nkubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm\n```\n### What happened?\n\nImporting the `litellm.proxy` (and probably other) modules crashes with the latest PyPI release. Worked up until the latest version of the package on PyPI.\n\nEdit: introduced inside https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/pull/10321."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'enterprise'",
+        "description": "> Lite LLM guys, PLEASE DO NOT BREAK STUFF in MINOR releases. I mean, if you want to move some essential modules to the enterprise, please first mark them somehow and do the official move in 1.68. I am among the numerous people who got everything broken just because of a minor litellm update.\n\nhi ,\n```yaml\n>>> import litellm.proxy\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"<python-input-0>\", line 1, in <module>\n    import litellm.proxy\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/__init__.py\", line 1058, in <module>\n    from .responses.main import *\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/main.py\", line 13, in <module>\n    from litellm.responses.litellm_completion_transformation.handler import (\n        LiteLLMCompletionTransformationHandler,\n    )\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/litellm_completion_transformation/handler.py\",\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade litellm to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade litellm:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n litellm\nhelm list -n litellm\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [Bug]: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> Lite LLM guys, PLEASE DO NOT BREAK STUFF in MINOR releases. I mean, if you want to move some essential modules to the enterprise, please first mark them somehow and do the official move in 1.68. I am among the numerous people who got everything broken just because of a minor litellm update.\n\nhi , acknowledging this feedback.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        ">>> import litellm.proxy\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"<python-input-0>\", line 1, in <module>\n    import litellm.proxy\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/__init__.py\", line 1058, in <module>\n    from .responses.main import *\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/main.py\", line 13, in <module>\n    from litellm.responses.litellm_completion_transformation.handler import (\n        LiteLLMCompletionTransformationHandler,\n    )\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/responses/litellm_completion_transformation/handler.py\", line 8, in <module>\n    from litellm.responses.litellm_completion_transformation.streaming_iterator import (\n        LiteLLMCompletionStreamingIterator,\n    )\n  File \"/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-p",
+        "pip3 index versions litellm\nWARNING: pip index is currently an experimental command. It may be removed/changed in a future release without prior warning.\nlitellm (1.67.4)\nAvailable versions: 1.67.4, 1.67.2, 1.67.1, 1.67.0.post1, 1.67.0, 1.66.3, 1.66.2, 1.66.1, 1.66.0, 1.65.8, 1.65.7, 1.65.6, 1.65.5, 1.65.4.post1 ...",
+        "litellm==1.67.4"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
